preamble: The development of [r] from [ʌ] is typically indirect, occurring through vowel reduction and epenthesis rather than direct replacement. When [ʌ] reduces to schwa [ə] at word boundaries or before another vowel, an intrusive [r] may be inserted (intrusive or linking R) to prevent hiatus in certain English dialects.

examples.note: Intrusive R appears when the STRUT vowel reduces and an epenthetic [r] is inserted before a following vowel to break hiatus.
